title: hygrophila glandulosa
intro: Hygrophila glandulosa, also known as the Indian watercress or bladderwort, is a species of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Hygrophila glandulosa include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Hygrophila glandulosa are often consumed as teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can be used to treat a variety of health issues due to its medicinal properties.
